steps.title: Identify what the passage claims about Pueblo sizes
steps.content:
The passage states that Laguna (~800 square miles) is the largest, and that most Pueblo tribal nations are much smaller. Sandia at 38.9 square miles is given as one example of this small scale. The value for Pueblo de Cochiti must also be consistent with "much smaller" than 800 square miles.
  • Choice A — '156.2 square miles.': 156.2 square miles is not the table value for Pueblo de Cochiti; it misidentifies the area. ✗
  • Choice B — '595.7 square miles.': 595.7 is close to Laguna's ~800, which would contradict the claim that most Pueblos are "much smaller." This does not come from Pueblo de Cochiti's row in the table. ✗
  • Choice C — '82.1 square miles.': 82.1 is the table value for Pueblo de Cochiti. It is much smaller than Laguna's ~800 square miles, consistent with the passage's claim. ✓
  • Choice D — '21.4 square miles.': 21.4 square miles is not the table value for Pueblo de Cochiti; it misidentifies the area. ✗

steps.title: Verify the value is both accurate and logically consistent
steps.content: The correct answer must (1) come from the Pueblo de Cochiti row in the table and (2) support the claim that most Pueblos are much smaller than Laguna. Only 82.1 satisfies both conditions. Choices A and D state values not found in Pueblo de Cochiti's row; choice B presents a value that would undermine the passage's point. That matches choice C — C is correct.
